behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from the recommendation results regardless of their relevance score.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is at the module boundary between stock-availability filtering and ranking/merge logic; a unit test of filter logic alone can miss reintroduction after ranking. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI-output dependence or multi-agent chain is described.
behaviors.test_description: Wire catalog fetch + availability filter + ranker in-process with a fixture where an out-of-stock item has the top relevance score; assert final emitted list never contains it.

behaviors.description: The user preference filter correctly excludes product categories the user has explicitly blocked in their settings.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is single-boundary filter logic: given blocked categories and products, exclusion should be deterministic. Rejected Integration because no concrete cross-module handoff is required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Test the preference-filter function with blocked-category fixtures (including case/ID normalization edge cases) and assert blocked categories are removed.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm ranks products that match the user's recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: This is scoring/ranking rule correctness inside the recommendation algorithm, not inherently an infrastructure or module-wiring failure. Rejected Agentic because behavior text describes algorithmic weighting, not nondeterministic model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Call the rank-scoring function with controlled feature vectors where one product has recent-browsing signal and another has none; assert higher score/rank for the former.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page of results, with stable ordering — requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if the underlying data hasn't changed.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when ranking output, tie-break ordering, and paginator interact; unit-testing paginator alone can miss unstable upstream ordering. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess.
behaviors.test_description: Run full in-process recommendation pipeline over fixed data with known ties, request page 2 twice without mutations, and assert same item IDs in same order both times.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category the user hasn't browsed before (serendipity requirement), avoiding a filter bubble.
behaviors.justification: Serendipity is enforced after combining filters, ranker, and diversification step; unit tests of a single scorer can miss final-list composition failures. Rejected Agentic because requirement is deterministic list-constraint behavior, not LLM output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute in-process pipeline with user history concentrated in one category and eligible items in multiple categories; assert final top-N contains at least one item from an unseen category.

behaviors.description: A new user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity rather than an empty result set.
behaviors.justification: Cold-start fallback depends on interaction between profile/history lookup and popularity-based candidate generation; unit-testing only one function can miss empty-output handoff bugs. Rejected System because no real OS/network/subprocess is required by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: With empty history inputs and a seeded popularity source module, run recommendation service end-to-end in-process and assert non-empty results ordered by popularity baseline.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records the correct product ID, position in the list, and timestamp for each recommendation the user interacts with.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is contract alignment between recommendation response context and tracking/persistence module (ID/position/timestamp mapping). Rejected System because local in-process modules plus local store are sufficient; no required external OS/network behavior in text.
behaviors.test_description: Serve recommendations, simulate a click on a specific ranked position, persist tracking event to local test store, and assert stored event fields (product_id, rank_position, timestamp within expected clock window).

behaviors.description: The response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end latency SLO; mocked dependencies can hide real serialization, memory, and runtime effects. Needs real process execution and wall-clock measurement at production-like scale.
behaviors.test_description: Run the recommendation service in a real process with a 100k-product dataset and realistic user-state inputs; measure p95/p99 wall-clock latency and fail if threshold exceeds 200ms.

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased by the user, the recommendation algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests.
behaviors.justification: Failure occurs at state handoff between purchase-event ingestion/history store and next recommendation ranking pass; unit tests on ranker alone can miss propagation issues. Rejected Agentic because behavior is deterministic state update/ranking effect.
behaviors.test_description: Generate initial recommendations, record purchase for a recommended item via purchase pipeline, rerun recommendations, and assert purchased item rank drops (or is excluded) versus prior result.

behaviors.description: The recommendation results are deterministic for the same user state — requesting recommendations twice with no intervening user activity returns the same ranked list.
behaviors.justification: Determinism failures often come from cross-module effects (unstable tie-breakers, cache/state leakage, unordered collection merges) across the pipeline, not a single pure function. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real HTTP/OS/subprocess.
behaviors.test_description: Run full in-process recommendation pipeline twice with identical user/catalog snapshots and fixed clock/random seed configuration; assert identical ordered product IDs across both calls.
